definition:
1. to be upon , with dative, (Iliad by Homer); but in Prose with preposition, ἐπὶ τοῦ καταστρώματος (Herdotus Historicus); ἐπὶ ταῖς οἰκίαις (Xenophon Historicus) 
2. to be set upon , (Herdotus Historicus):—;of rewards and penalties, to be affixed  or attached , (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc. 
3. of Time, to be hereafter, remain , (Odyssey by Homer); ἐπεσσόμενοι ἄνθρωποι generations to come , (Oracle quoted in Herdotus Historicus):—;also to be at hand , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
4. to be set over , Lat. praeesse, τισι (Herdotus Historicus) 
5. to be added, be over and above , of numbers, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
